<details>
<summary>📝 Walkthrough</summary>

## Walkthrough

This PR refactors the development tooling and CI/CD infrastructure to consolidate npm scripts and workflows. It introduces helper scripts that intelligently process only changed files for formatting and affected tests, updates GitHub Actions workflows and the pre-commit hook to use the new script wrappers instead of direct tool invocations, and documents the new verification flow in the README. Additionally, the PR makes targeted application-code updates: migrating email types to use the current nodemailer API, moving auth signup configuration to the client module, conditionally gating error details based on an `exposeDetails` flag, normalizing genre query input, and making database test migrations dynamically discoverable.

</details>

<!-- walkthrough_end -->
<!-- pre_merge_checks_walkthrough_start -->

<summary>🚥 Pre-merge checks | ✅ 2 | ❌ 1</summary>

| Title check | ⚠️ Warning | The PR title is incomplete and truncated, ending with 'to allow o…' without conveying the full scope of changes. While it references .semgrep.yml, the PR encompasses significant changes across 12 files including workflow updates, script management, module imports, and configuration restructuring. | Revise the title to be more comprehensive and complete, such as: 'Refactor scripts, tighten imports, and standardize npm/CI workflow commands' or 'Consolidate build scripts and improve module import validation'. |

## Code Review

This pull request introduces a more robust local verification workflow by adding scripts to format and test only affected files, updating the `package.json` scripts, and refining the `pre-commit` hooks. Feedback highlights that the `listChangedFiles` function in the new scripts fails to account for staged files, which is critical for `pre-commit` hooks. Additionally, the changes to `lefthook.yml` remove the automatic staging of fixed files and shift the linting process from staged files to the entire project, which may negatively impact developer productivity and commit speed.

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The updated `pre-commit` configuration has a few drawbacks compared to the previous implementation:

1. **Missing `stage_fixed: true`**: Since `format:changed` modifies files in-place, those changes won't be automatically added back to the current commit. This means the commit will likely contain unformatted code unless the user manually stages the changes again after the hook runs.
2. **Full Project Linting**: `pnpm lint` now runs `oxlint .`, which lints the entire project. This is significantly slower than linting only staged files and can block commits due to existing issues in unrelated files, which can be frustrating for developers.

Consider reverting to using `{staged_files}` with `stage_fixed: true` for `pre-commit` hooks to maintain a fast and reliable developer workflow, and keep the "affected" scripts for pre-push or CI gates.

🟡 **Medium** `workflows/code-quality.yml:21` The change from `pnpm oxlint --deny-warnings .` to `pnpm lint` removes `--deny-warnings`, so oxlint now exits 0 even when warnings exist. Several rules (e.g., `simple-import-sort/exports`, `@tanstack/query/no-rest-destructuring`, `sonarjs/cognitive-complexity`, `sonarjs/todo-tag`) are configured at warn level in `.oxlintrc.json` and will no longer fail CI. Consider adding `--deny-warnings` back to the lint command so warnings are treated as errors in CI. ```diff - name: Run oxlint - run: pnpm lint + run: pnpm lint -- --deny-warnings ```
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent: ```text In file .github/workflows/code-quality.yml around lines 21-22: The change from `pnpm oxlint --deny-warnings .` to `pnpm lint` removes `--deny-warnings`, so oxlint now exits 0 even when warnings exist. Several rules (e.g., `simple-import-sort/exports`, `@tanstack/query/no-rest-destructuring`, `sonarjs/cognitive-complexity`, `sonarjs/todo-tag`) are configured at warn level in `.oxlintrc.json` and will no longer fail CI. Consider adding `--deny-warnings` back to the lint command so warnings are treated as errors in CI. ```

**4 issues found** across 13 files
Prompt for AI agents (unresolved issues) ```text Check if these issues are valid — if so, understand the root cause of each and fix them. If appropriate, use sub-agents to investigate and fix each issue separately. P2: Staged-only files are omitted from formatting because the script does not include `git diff --cached` when collecting changed files. P1: `listChangedFiles` ignores staged changes (`--cached`), so affected tests can be skipped for staged files during local/hook runs. P1: `pnpm lint` drops `--deny-warnings` that was previously used, so oxlint warnings will no longer fail CI. P1: Missing `stage_fixed: true` on `format` command. The old `check` command used `stage_fixed: true` because `oxfmt` writes formatting changes in-place. The new `format` command runs `oxfmt` (via `pnpm format:changed`) but omits `stage_fixed`, so the formatted code won't be re-staged — the commit will contain unformatted code while the formatted version sits as unstaged changes. ```
Reply with feedback, questions, or to request a fix.

P1: Missing `stage_fixed: true` on `format` command. The old `check` command used `stage_fixed: true` because `oxfmt` writes formatting changes in-place. The new `format` command runs `oxfmt` (via `pnpm format:changed`) but omits `stage_fixed`, so the formatted code won't be re-staged — the commit will contain unformatted code while the formatted version sits as unstaged changes.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI agents</summary>

```text
Check if this issue is valid — if so, understand the root cause and fix it. At lefthook.yml, line 3:

<comment>Missing `stage_fixed: true` on `format` command. The old `check` command used `stage_fixed: true` because `oxfmt` writes formatting changes in-place. The new `format` command runs `oxfmt` (via `pnpm format:changed`) but omits `stage_fixed`, so the formatted code won't be re-staged — the commit will contain unformatted code while the formatted version sits as unstaged changes.</comment>

<file context>
@@ -1,13 +1,9 @@
-      glob: '*.{js,ts,cjs,mjs,jsx,tsx}'
-      run: pnpm oxlint --fix {staged_files}
-      stage_fixed: true
+    format:
+      run: pnpm format:changed
+    lint:
</file context>
```

</details>

```suggestion
    format:
      run: pnpm format:changed
      stage_fixed: true
```
